verses.text: We that are strong ought 'to bear the infirmities of the weak,' not to please ourselves. Bearing love. Strength serves, not pleases, itself.
We that are strong ought 'to bear the infirmities of the weak,' not to please ourselves. Bearing love. Strength serves, not pleases, itself.
We that are strong ought 'to bear the infirmities of the weak,' not to please ourselves. Bearing love. Strength serves, not pleases, itself.
Whatever was written aforetime was 'for our learning,' that we 'might have hope.' Scripture's use. The word feeds patience and hope.
Whatever was written aforetime was 'for our learning,' that we 'might have hope.' Scripture's use. The word feeds patience and hope.
Whatever was written aforetime was 'for our learning,' that we 'might have hope.' Scripture's use. The word feeds patience and hope.
Receive one another, 'as Christ also received us'; He confirmed the promises, that the Gentiles might glorify God. Welcoming grace. Christ's welcome is the pattern of ours.
Receive one another, 'as Christ also received us'; He confirmed the promises, that the Gentiles might glorify God. Welcoming grace. Christ's welcome is the pattern of ours.
Receive one another, 'as Christ also received us'; He confirmed the promises, that the Gentiles might glorify God. Welcoming grace. Christ's welcome is the pattern of ours.
Rejoice, ye Gentiles; 'the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing.' Filled with hope. Faith brings joy, peace, and abounding hope.
Rejoice, ye Gentiles; 'the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing.' Filled with hope. Faith brings joy, peace, and abounding hope.

Paul, 'a minister... to the Gentiles,' writes boldly, that their offering be acceptable. His calling. He labours to present the nations to God.
Paul, 'a minister... to the Gentiles,' writes boldly, that their offering be acceptable. His calling. He labours to present the nations to God.
Paul, 'a minister... to the Gentiles,' writes boldly, that their offering be acceptable. His calling. He labours to present the nations to God.
He speaks only of what Christ wrought by him, 'through mighty signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit.' All of Christ. He takes no glory to himself.
He speaks only of what Christ wrought by him, 'through mighty signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit.' All of Christ. He takes no glory to himself.
He speaks only of what Christ wrought by him, 'through mighty signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit.' All of Christ. He takes no glory to himself.
He strove to preach where Christ was not named, lest he build on another's foundation. Pioneer. He sought the unreached with the gospel.
He strove to preach where Christ was not named, lest he build on another's foundation. Pioneer. He sought the unreached with the gospel.
He purposes to visit them on his way to Spain. Travel plans. He longs to see and be helped on his journey by them.
He purposes to visit them on his way to Spain. Travel plans. He longs to see and be helped on his journey by them.

First he goes to Jerusalem with a gift for 'the poor saints,' a debt the Gentiles owe. Loving relief. Grateful Gentiles share with needy Jews.

First he goes to Jerusalem with a gift for 'the poor saints,' a debt the Gentiles owe. Loving relief. Grateful Gentiles share with needy Jews.
When this is done, he will come 'in the fulness of the blessing... of Christ.' Coming blessing. He expects to bring Christ's fullness with him.
When this is done, he will come 'in the fulness of the blessing... of Christ.' Coming blessing. He expects to bring Christ's fullness with him.
Strive with me in prayers, that I be delivered; 'the God of peace be with you all.' Asking prayer. Even the apostle leans on the prayers of the saints.
